rawCommits.body:
Suppress the brief "Select a project to get started" render while project
actions are still being registered on a project route, and make the Run
pane header match the height of the in-line specs search (32px).

rawCommits.subject: Mirror specs to disk and show filename-style tool card
rawCommits.body:
Render create_spec / update_spec tool calls with a file-style card
(<slug>.md header + streamed markdown body) and dual-write specs to
<workspace_root>/spec/<slug>.md in addition to existing DB persistence,
so the spec that's being generated is both visible in the chat and
available on the project's filesystem.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Mirror specs to disk and show filename-style tool card

rawCommits.subject: Fade tool card body out before collapsing
rawCommits.body:
Pair the existing fadeSlideIn with a fadeSlideOut keyframe and a
.toolBodyCollapsing class so ToolCallBlock bodies dissolve over ~120ms
instead of disappearing in one frame. This removes the blink and the
bottom-overflow flash that was visible when a card auto-collapsed on
pending-to-done (create_spec, update_spec, and other tool cards).

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Fade tool card body out before collapsing

rawCommits.subject: Show file previews while file-op tool calls are streaming
rawCommits.body:
Mirrors the existing spec-preview streaming behavior for write_file,
edit_file, read_file, and delete_file so users can see the file path
and partial content (or a spinner) inline before the tool completes.
Adds delete_file to FILE_OPS with a dedicated header-only card.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Show file previews while file-op tool calls are streaming

rawCommits.subject: Stabilize create_spec tool card mount
rawCommits.body:
Render SpecPreviewCard unconditionally for pending create_spec / update_spec
tool calls (the card already handles the empty streaming state via its own
spinner), so the body keeps a stable shape from first paint instead of
briefly showing the generic "Waiting for the tool result" JSON block and
then swapping to the spec card once the title streams in.

Also drop the -4px translateY from fadeSlideIn so the body doesn't paint
offset from its reserved box during the 150ms enter animation. Together
these remove the visible jerk where a newly appearing tool card flashed
above/below where it should sit in the chat lane.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Stabilize create_spec tool card mount

rawCommits.subject: Center chat input on empty threads
rawCommits.body:
When an agent has no conversation history, render the input bar
vertically centered in the chat lane and slide it down to its docked
position once the first message is sent. Uses matching `bottom` +
`transform` in both states so the transition animates smoothly.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Center chat input on empty threads

rawCommits.subject: Stream create_spec markdown into the tool card live
rawCommits.body:
While super-agent tool_use blocks were streaming from Claude, input_json_delta
chunks were accumulated server-side but never surfaced to the client, so the
spec preview card sat on a spinner until the tool finished and the markdown
finally arrived in one jump.

Server: a small partial_json helper extracts the in-flight value of a known
top-level string field (title / markdown_contents) from the accumulator
buffer, respecting JSON escape sequences. SuperAgentStream now emits a
throttled ToolCallSnapshot (~50ms floor) for create_spec / update_spec on
each input_json_delta and a final snapshot at content_block_stop, so the
client sees the spec grow in real time. No protocol change --
ToolCallSnapshot already existed and was already merged into
ToolCallEntry.input by handleToolCallSnapshot.

UI: SpecPreviewCard is now a fixed 3-line viewport (height based on 1.5em *
3 + padding) pinned to the bottom while the call is pending, so the most
recently written lines stay in view like a rolling terminal tail. Dropped
the old 20-line slice and the "Show all / Show less" toggle so the card
keeps a stable shape while streaming. Added focused tests for the
auto-scroll behavior and 11 unit tests for the partial JSON extractor.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Stream create_spec markdown into the tool card live

rawCommits.subject: Fix zero Projects/Signups/Revenue tiles in platform stats
rawCommits.body:
The PlatformStats deserializer only accepted camelCase keys, so any field
the remote aura-network emitted under a different name (e.g. snake_case
or a synonym like projectCount) silently defaulted to 0 via
#[serde(default)]. Added snake_case and common alternate-name aliases
for each field so the decoder is robust to the remote's actual key
names, and log the raw /api/stats response body once so we can verify
which keys are in use.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Fix zero Projects/Signups/Revenue tiles in platform stats

rawCommits.subject: Use larger desktop runners and switch changelog to Pacific time
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Use larger desktop runners and switch changelog to Pacific time

rawCommits.subject: Use totalCostUsd for Total Revenue tile
rawCommits.body:
Remote /api/stats returns the platform revenue under the key
totalCostUsd, and the today-signup count under newSignupsToday.
Added those and a few cost-flavored aliases to PlatformStats so
the Total Revenue and New Signups tiles decode real values instead
of silently defaulting to 0. Renamed the Projects and Revenue
tiles to Total Projects / Total Revenue to match the upstream
metric scope (cumulative, not daily), and demoted the now-verified
raw-body trace to debug so we don't log response bodies at info
in steady state.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Use totalCostUsd for Total Revenue tile

rawCommits.subject: Move logout into team settings panel, drop app settings modal
rawCommits.body:
Removes the top-bar Settings gear in DesktopShell, along with the entire
SettingsModal (app updates + support links + logout lived here) and the
corresponding mobile "App settings" entry points. Logout now surfaces as
a persistent action pinned to the bottom of the OrgSettingsPanel left
nav so it's reachable from every team-settings tab.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Move logout into team settings panel, drop app settings modal

rawCommits.subject: Eliminate post-stream jerk when the assistant bubble finalizes
rawCommits.body:
The StreamingBubble showed the ThinkingRow expanded; the new MessageBubble
swapped in with it collapsed, and ChatMessageList stuffed the streaming
bubble's tall height into the virtualizer cache for the new message. That
triggered a second scroll reconcile once the ResizeObserver measured the
actually-short bubble, producing a visible upward jerk.

Mark the just-finalized message so its ThinkingRow mounts expanded (via a
new defaultExpanded / initialThinkingExpanded / defaultThinkingExpanded
prop chain through LLMOutput, ActivityTimeline, and MessageBubble). The
swap is now a visual no-op, and the existing auto-collapse happens one
render later as a single clean layout change after the bubble has been
measured. Drop the streaming-height cache override since it is no longer
needed.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Eliminate post-stream jerk when the assistant bubble finalizes

rawCommits.subject: Make leaderboard global; key Organization filter to active org
rawCommits.body:
Platform Stats are global (49 users, 19.1M tokens) while the
leaderboard was unconditionally org-scoped, so it only showed the
active org's slice (e.g. n3o at 45K). The feed's Organization
filter was also a no-op that returned every event because org_id
was dropped when building FeedEvent from the DTO.

Drop the unused filter/setFilter state and the activeOrg
subscription from leaderboard-store so fetchEntries always calls
/api/leaderboard without org_id. Surface orgId on FeedEvent and
populate it from FeedEventDto.org_id, then have applyFilter honor
the current org for the Organization tab (falls back to empty when
no org is active). Everything/Leaderboard views remain global.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Make leaderboard global; key Organization filter to active org

rawCommits.subject: Remove events column from leaderboard
rawCommits.body:
The events counter showed the aura-network activity_event count
per profile (git pushes, posts, comments), which is easy to read
as "AI activity" and is not comparable to the token totals next
to it. Pull it from both the leaderboard list row and the user
sidekick card until we have a clearer signal to put there.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Remove events column from leaderboard

rawCommits.subject: Stabilize EntityCard header height and Sidekick label colors
rawCommits.body:
Always render the EntityCard name-action slot with a fixed min
width/height so rows with a Follow/Edit button and rows without
one line up to the same 56px header height; the slot is hidden
when no action is supplied. Move the header's bottom border onto
the body so the divider only shows when slotted content exists.

Tighten the Sidekick body typography to 12px with line-height
1.25 and switch parent collapsible items to the
--color-text-secondary shade that the Projects LeftMenu uses,
keyed on aria-expanded="true"/"false" explicitly so leaf items
stay white. Add a small EntityCard test covering the presence and
absence of the name-action placeholder.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Stabilize EntityCard header height and Sidekick label colors

rawCommits.subject: Draft specs visibly before persisting them
rawCommits.body:
Guide the super-agent to stream spec drafts as normal assistant text before
calling create_spec/update_spec, then seed pending spec tool cards from that
just-streamed draft via input.draft_preview. When the real
markdown_contents snapshots arrive, the card naturally hands off from the
fallback draft to the authoritative tool input.

Also flush any pending streamed text before starting a spec tool so the user
sees the completed visible draft instead of a spinner-only card, and cover
both the handler and preview handoff behavior with focused tests.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Draft specs visibly before persisting them

rawCommits.subject: Count any feed event with commit metadata in CommitGrid
rawCommits.body:
The feed page commit grid was always empty because getCommitCount gated
on postType === "push", but createPost hardcodes post_type="post", so no
persisted events ever satisfied the gate. Drop the gate and count any
event whose commits[] or commit_ids[] is populated, regardless of post
type.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Count any feed event with commit metadata in CommitGrid

rawCommits.subject: Render unwrapped delete error messages in project/agent flows
rawCommits.body:
The project-delete and agent-instance-delete hooks were reading
err.body.error directly, which contains the upstream proxy's raw
JSON blob (e.g. {"error":{"code":"BAD_REQUEST","message":"Bad
request: Cannot delete project with existing project agents..."}}).
Route these catch blocks through the shared getApiErrorMessage /
getApiErrorDetails helpers so the modal shows the actual message,
matching the global agent-delete flow.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Render unwrapped delete error messages in project/agent flows

rawCommits.subject: Smooth tool/thinking collapse so pinned chat stops blinking upward
rawCommits.body:
At end of stream the ThinkingRow and ToolCallBlock bodies were unmounted
in a single frame, which (while pinned to bottom) caused every row above
the collapsed element to visually jump up. Fix by (a) never auto-collapsing
either row when streaming ends so the finalized bubble matches the last
streaming frame, and (b) animating user-driven collapse via a
grid-template-rows 0fr<->1fr transition on the wrapper. The existing
ResizeObserver-driven pin-to-bottom correction now tracks each
intermediate height before paint, turning the collapse into a gentle
slide instead of a one-frame blink. Just-finalized MessageBubble tool
rows receive initialActivitiesExpanded so the StreamingBubble ->
MessageBubble swap is a visual no-op.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Smooth tool/thinking collapse so pinned chat stops blinking upward

rawCommits.cleanSubject: Fix Linux larger-runner desktop dependencies

rawCommits.subject: Repair markdown strong emphasis with trailing whitespace before closer
rawCommits.body:
Models sometimes emit `**Title **` with a stray space before the closing
marker, which CommonMark refuses to recognize as right-flanking, so the
asterisks render literally in chat. Extend normalizeLooseStrongEmphasis
to also fix trailing-only whitespace cases while leaving well-formed
`**bold**` spans untouched.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Repair markdown strong emphasis with trailing whitespace before closer

rawCommits.subject: Show task title in create_task rows and make expand useful
rawCommits.body:
Pending create_task rows no longer stay stuck on "Generating..." once
the title has streamed in, and the expanded body renders the task title
(plus description when present) instead of an empty panel. Tightens the
vertical padding between stacked tool rows so a long checklist reads as
a dense list rather than a spaced-out column.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Show task title in create_task rows and make expand useful

rawCommits.subject: Add Linux toolchain packages on larger runners
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Add Linux toolchain packages on larger runners

rawCommits.subject: Stream partial tool inputs into spec and file preview cards
rawCommits.body:
Extend the super-agent harness to emit `tool_call_snapshot` events for
`write_file` and `edit_file` alongside the existing spec tools, parsing
their `path` / `content` / `old_text` / `new_text` fields out of the
in-flight `input_json_delta` buffer. The preview cards now render the
real text the model is writing character-by-character instead of a
spinner: `SpecPreviewCard` always mounts its code area with a blinking
caret while pending, `FilePreviewCard` drops the pending spinner in
favor of streaming `DiffView` / `CodeView`, and `ToolRow` no longer
shows a redundant "Generating..." header hint for spec or file tools.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Stream partial tool inputs into spec and file preview cards

rawCommits.subject: Tighten spacing between tool-only assistant bubbles
rawCommits.body:
Each iteration of the agent tool-use loop lands in its own saved
assistant event, so a 13-step "delete 13 specs" turn became 13 separate
MessageBubbles with 16px of bubble padding plus a 12px virtualizer gap
between each row. Tool rows within one bubble were already flush, but
across bubbles they drifted apart, giving the checklist an
inconsistent, airy feel.

Drop the vertical padding on assistant bubbles that carry only tool
calls (no prose, no thinking, no artifact refs) via a new
bubbleAssistantCompact modifier, and trim the ChatMessageList
virtualizer gap from 12px to 6px so text and tool rows sit at a
consistent rhythm across the board.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Tighten spacing between tool-only assistant bubbles

rawCommits.subject: Only auto-expand tool rows with live preview content
rawCommits.body:
Finalized activity bubbles were dumping raw input/result JSON under
list/get/delete/transition rows because every non-create_task tool was
auto-expanded on finalize. Narrow auto-expansion to the handful of
tools whose body actually streams useful content -- create_spec,
update_spec, write_file, edit_file, run_command -- so reads, lists,
deletes and task CRUD stay collapsed and the checklist reads as a
summary rather than a wall of JSON.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Only auto-expand tool rows with live preview content

rawCommits.subject: Add right-click delete for specs and tasks in Sidekick
rawCommits.body:
The Specs and Tasks panels render the zui Explorer directly with no
context-menu wiring, so right-click did nothing. Add a reusable
SidekickItemContextMenu hook/component, expose deleteSpec/deleteTask in
the API client (endpoints already existed server-side), and wire both
list views to optimistically remove the target and roll back on error.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Add right-click delete for specs and tasks in Sidekick

rawCommits.subject: Show real per-project token usage from aura-network
rawCommits.body:
The project stats grid was reporting 0 tokens and $0 cost because
aura-storage's /api/stats?scope=project endpoint is only populated for
automaton runs (dev_loop calls report_usage explicitly). Chat turns go
through aura-router, which already tracks per-project usage and exposes
it on aura-network's /internal/projects/:projectId/usage endpoint.

Add internal-token auth to NetworkClient and overlay the authoritative
tokens/cost from aura-network on top of the storage stats response.
Also wire the super-agent GetProjectCostTool to the real endpoint so it
stops claiming per-project cost tracking is unavailable.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Show real per-project token usage from aura-network

rawCommits.subject: Enable Anthropic fine-grained tool streaming for spec/file tools
rawCommits.body:
Without the `anthropic-beta: fine-grained-tool-streaming-2025-05-14`
header and the per-tool `eager_input_streaming: true` flag, Anthropic
validates the tool-use JSON as it streams and only emits
`input_json_delta` events for complete fragments. That meant the
`markdown_contents` of a `create_spec` call — a single very long
string value — was buffered until the tool block closed, so the
`SpecPreviewCard` would show only the pre-tool draft preamble with a
blinking caret until the whole spec finished generating, then snap to
the full markdown at once.

Opting both sides into fine-grained streaming makes Anthropic emit
`input_json_delta` chunks as raw partial string bytes, which the
existing `extract_partial_string_field` / `build_partial_spec_input`
path already knows how to parse and forward as `tool_call_snapshot`
events. Consolidates the streaming tool-name list into a single shared
`is_streaming_tool_name` helper so the snapshot emitter and the
`eager_input_streaming` tool-definition flag can't drift.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Enable Anthropic fine-grained tool streaming for spec/file tools

rawCommits.subject: Add OpenSSL dev headers on Linux larger runners
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Add OpenSSL dev headers on Linux larger runners

rawCommits.subject: Tighten tool row spacing further across bubble boundaries
rawCommits.body:
Three sources were still adding visible space between successive tool
rows: assistant turns that emit an empty text block alongside a
tool_use were never classified as tool-only (so the bubble kept its
16px padding), the ChatMessageList virtualizer still inserted 6px
between every bubble, and each tool header carried 2px of vertical
padding.

Count whitespace-only content blocks as "no prose" when deciding
bubbleAssistantCompact, drop MESSAGE_GAP to 2px, and trim
.toolHeader padding to 1px 0. Tool rows within a bubble now sit at
~15px pitch, and tool rows across bubbles sit at ~17px, so the
checklist reads as a single continuous list regardless of whether the
agent batched its tool calls into one assistant turn or many.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Tighten tool row spacing further across bubble boundaries

rawCommits.subject: Filter deleted tasks and specs from TaskList view
rawCommits.body:
removeTask only dropped items from the sidekick store's tasks array, but
useTaskListData merges that with localTasks (initial fetch + streamed
events), so right-click delete had no visible effect on the Tasks panel.
Deleting a spec from the Tasks view had the same issue because
useTaskListData didn't apply deletedSpecIds either.

Mirror the deletedSpecIds pattern for tasks (deletedTaskIds + removeTask
tracks, pushTask clears, clearDeletedTasks resets on refetch), and apply
both deletion filters in useTaskListData. Also restore the item via
pushSpec/pushTask on delete failure in both list views.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Filter deleted tasks and specs from TaskList view

rawCommits.subject: Log tool streaming flow to diagnose live preview lag
rawCommits.body:
Add `info!` / `debug!` tracing at each step of the tool-use streaming
pipeline so we can tell whether Anthropic is actually emitting
`input_json_delta` events in real time (fine-grained streaming active)
or buffering them until the block closes (fine-grained streaming
disabled — e.g. because the billing router is stripping the
`eager_input_streaming` flag out of the tool definitions).

- On every `/v1/messages` request: log the list of tool names that
  carry `eager_input_streaming: true` so we can confirm the flag is
  still present on the outbound body.
- On `tool_use` content_block_start: log the tool name + whether the
  harness considers it a streaming tool.
- On every `input_json_delta` chunk: debug-log the delta length and
  accumulated buffer size.
- On every emitted `ToolCallSnapshot`: debug-log the current
  `markdown_contents` / `content` length.
- On `tool_use` content_block_stop: log total bytes and whether any
  mid-stream snapshots were emitted before the final one.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Log tool streaming flow to diagnose live preview lag

rawCommits.subject: Stop reformatting assistant bubbles after stream finishes
rawCommits.body:
The streaming bubble rendered LLM output without the `.markdown` CSS scope,
but the finalized MessageBubble wrapped it in `<div className={styles.markdown}>`
which overrode heading sizes, paragraph spacing, list/table/code/link/blockquote
styling. The moment the stream ended the content visibly reflowed.

Drop the `.markdown` wrapper (and its CSS block) so finalized assistant
messages render with the same plain `.bubbleAssistant` scope as the live
streaming bubble, eliminating the post-stream formatting shift.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Stop reformatting assistant bubbles after stream finishes

rawCommits.subject: Rename Swarm environment label to Remote in agent create form
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Rename Swarm environment label to Remote in agent create form

rawCommits.subject: Add aura_project_id to SessionInit for router billing headers
rawCommits.body:
aura-harness's reasoner sends X-Aura-Project-Id on every proxied /v1/messages
when its ModelRequest has aura_project_id set, which is how aura-router
attributes per-project usage on POST /internal/usage. The SessionInit protocol
already carries aura_agent_id / aura_session_id / aura_org_id for the matching
headers, but had no dedicated aura_project_id field (only project_id, which
the harness uses for domain tool routing). Add the missing field and forward
it through SessionConfig and both harness backends so the caller can populate
the billing header.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Add aura_project_id to SessionInit for router billing headers

rawCommits.subject: Preserve tool-only assistant turns in chat history on reopen
rawCommits.body:
Standalone agent chat was losing all prior assistant/tool turns when the
app was closed and reopened, so the LLM restarted each session without
memory of anything it had done. User turns survived because they take a
separate code path.

Three silent-drop points were stripping assistant turns from the history
that feeds the model on cold start:

- events_to_session_history used serde_json::from_value::<Vec<_>>(..).ok()
  so a single unknown or malformed ChatContentBlock variant nuked the
  whole vector; combined with the empty-content guard this dropped the
  entire turn. Switch to per-block deserialization that logs and skips
  only the bad block.
- session_events_to_conversation_history filtered on empty content, so
  any tool-only assistant turn (empty text, populated content_blocks)
  was removed before it reached the harness. Render tool_use /
  tool_result / refs into the flat text shape the harness expects so
  the LLM still sees prior tool activity.
- spawn_chat_persist_task aborted the turn on RecvError::Lagged and on
  early channel close, losing the terminating assistant_message_end
  and therefore the full turn. Continue draining on lag, fill null
  tool_use inputs from the first tool_result, and synthesize a final
  end event when the broadcast closes before the harness signals
  end-of-turn.

Covered by new unit tests for tool-only round-trip, unknown block
tolerance, and the conversation-history renderer.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Preserve tool-only assistant turns in chat history on reopen

rawCommits.subject: Revert "Add aura_project_id to SessionInit for router billing headers"
rawCommits.body: This reverts commit cfdd29d4167b9b1cb83cdea36135a8b7b1b5f666.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Revert "Add aura_project_id to SessionInit for router billing headers"

rawCommits.subject: Refetch project stats when a chat stream ends
rawCommits.body:
Previously useStatsDashboardData only fetched on mount / projectId change,
so tokens, cost, and events stayed frozen after sending a message even
though the backend had already recorded the turn. Subscribe to the shared
stream store and refetch on the falling edge of any entry's isStreaming
flag, so the grid updates the moment an LLM turn completes.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Refetch project stats when a chat stream ends

rawCommits.subject: Sum input+output tokens when aura-network totalTokens is 0
rawCommits.body:
The project stats grid showed $0.11 cost alongside 0 tokens because some
aura-network deployments return a populated totalCostUsd / totalInputTokens
/ totalOutputTokens but leave the aggregate totalTokens column at 0. Treat
totalTokens as authoritative only when it's non-zero, otherwise fall back
to the component columns so the UI stays consistent with cost. Also log
the raw response at debug level to help diagnose future mismatches.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Sum input+output tokens when aura-network totalTokens is 0

rawCommits.subject: Log aura-network project usage response at info level
rawCommits.body:
Upgrading the project-stats diagnostic from debug! to info! so the
response is visible in default logging, and adding an explicit branch
log when the internal token is missing or the network client is disabled.
Makes it obvious whether 0-token grids are caused by a configuration gap,
a network fetch failure, or a backend returning inconsistent fields.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Log aura-network project usage response at info level

rawCommits.subject: Sum input+output tokens when aura-storage totalTokens is 0
rawCommits.body:
Your logs show `aura-network internal token not configured`, which means
the overlay from the previous commit never runs and tokens/cost both come
from aura-storage. aura-storage returns a populated `estimatedCostUsd`
($0.11) alongside `totalTokens = 0`, mirroring the same aggregate-column
quirk we already worked around on the aura-network side.

Add `totalInputTokens` / `totalOutputTokens` (both optional) to the
`ProjectStats` shape and fall back to their sum in the From impl when the
top-level aggregate is 0. Also log the raw storage response at info level
to make the next mismatch visible without tweaking RUST_LOG.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Sum input+output tokens when aura-storage totalTokens is 0

rawCommits.subject: Remove aura-network internal-token paths from the client
rawCommits.body:
Internal tokens are service-to-service secrets and don't belong in the
desktop client. Strip NetworkClient::has_internal_token, the internal-only
/internal/projects/:projectId/usage call, the ProjectUsage type, and the
aura-network overlay in the project stats handler. Revert
GetProjectCostTool to its pre-internal-token placeholder.

Per-project token totals now come purely from aura-storage (authed with
the user JWT), with the existing totalInputTokens / totalOutputTokens
fallback from the previous commit handling the case where aura-storage
returns a zero aggregate alongside populated component columns.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Remove aura-network internal-token paths from the client

rawCommits.subject: Restore missing user and assistant turns in project chat history
rawCommits.body:
Project agents were reopening with only a subset of prior messages
because three independent paths silently dropped turns:

- `user_message` rows used an all-or-nothing `Vec<ChatContentBlock>`
  deserialize. Any single unknown/legacy block (common for image-only
  turns) cleared the whole vector; combined with the display filter
  dropping messages that had no content and no blocks, image-only user
  turns vanished on reopen. Use the same per-block tolerant helper as
  the assistant branch so known blocks survive even when peers fail.

- `storage_session_sort_key` compared timestamps as raw strings, so
  mixed RFC3339 formats (e.g. `...Z` vs `...+00:00` with fractional
  seconds) sorted incorrectly and the reader could pick a stale
  session. Parse timestamps with `DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339` and
  fall back to the Unix epoch so sessions without a recency signal
  always lose to sessions that have one.

- The chat persistence task broke out of its loop on
  `HarnessOutbound::Error` and only synthesized an
  `assistant_message_end` when some output had accumulated. Turns
  that errored before any output (auth, credits, provider 4xx on
  first byte) stored the user message but no assistant reply, so
  every reopen showed a dangling user turn. Synthesize a minimal
  terminating row with the error summary so the turn is recoverable.

Also align the writer's session selection in `resolve_chat_session`
with the reader by sorting on the shared recency key, so new events
never land in a different session than the next read will pick. Add
diagnostic logs in `load_project_session_history` with session count,
picked session id, and a breakdown of user/assistant event types so
future regressions can be diagnosed without adding instrumentation.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Restore missing user and assistant turns in project chat history

rawCommits.subject: Keep prior messages visible after starting a new chat session
rawCommits.body:
The reset-session button used to clear the chat view locally (history
cache + stream events), so users perceived it as deleting their
conversation even though the server was preserving the old session rows
and creating a fresh one.

Client: handleNewSession in AgentChatView (both standalone and project
panels) and use-agent-chat-window no longer calls clearHistory or
resetEvents. Prior events stay on screen; only the context-usage meter
resets and the next send is marked new_session so it lands in the new
storage session.

Server: load_latest_agent_events_from_storage_result and
load_project_session_history now iterate every session for the agent /
agent instance, reconstruct each session independently via
events_to_session_history, and concatenate them oldest-first. On reload
the UI sees the full multi-session transcript instead of only the
latest session. latest_storage_session is retained for its unit tests
(it pins down the storage_session_sort_key ordering contract) and gated
to #[cfg(test)].

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Keep prior messages visible after starting a new chat session

rawCommits.subject: Preserve terminal task status against stale task_saved events
rawCommits.body:
A `task_saved` broadcast from the agent runtime can arrive after
`task_completed`/`task_failed` with a stale `in_progress` snapshot,
because the status transition in storage happens on a separate code
path from the dev loop's completion handling. Without a guard, the
frontend's task list (sidekick store + useTaskListData) overwrites the
just-applied terminal status, so a task reads "Done" in the Run panel
but reverts to an open circle in the Tasks view while the loop is
still running.

Fix the race on both sides:

- Frontend: make `done`/`failed` sticky in `useSidekickStore.pushTask`
  and `useTaskListData.upsertTask`. A non-terminal `task_saved` payload
  is merged but cannot downgrade an already-terminal status, and keeps
  the local `execution_notes`/`files_changed` when the incoming payload
  hasn't caught up yet.

- Backend: in the dev loop's `task_completed` / `task_failed` branches,
  call `storage.transition_task(Done|Failed)` after persisting output
  so any subsequent `task_saved` snapshot already carries the terminal
  status. Transition errors (e.g. already terminal) are logged and
  non-fatal.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Preserve terminal task status against stale task_saved events

rawCommits.subject: Make Stop automation resilient to harness failures
rawCommits.body:
The Stop button would leave the UI stuck on Pause/Stop (and the
loop potentially running) whenever the stop call failed or the
registry was already empty:

- The backend's `stop_loop` returned 400 when nothing matched and
  502 when every `client.stop()` call errored, leaving the
  automaton_registry populated with unreachable entries that
  blocked future starts and stops.
- The frontend's `handleStopConfirm` swallowed errors to the
  console and only updated local state inside the `try`, so a
  failed stop never toggled the buttons back to Run and never
  told the user what went wrong.

Fix both sides:

- Backend: make `stop_loop` idempotent. An empty target set now
  returns 200 with the current status, and harness-side errors no
  longer abort the loop - we log the failure but still remove the
  registry entry, emit `loop_stopped`, and persist the log event
  so subscribers reconcile. Exported `ActiveAutomaton` to let the
  integration tests seed the registry.

- Frontend: `handleStopConfirm` optimistically clears
  `activeAgents`/`paused`/`starting`/`preparing` before awaiting
  the HTTP call, then reconciles against the authoritative status
  on both success and failure. Failures surface via a new
  `stopError` modal (dismissable) instead of being silently
  dropped. `handlePause` also clears `preparing` on success.

Tests:
- Rename `loop_stop_without_running` to
  `loop_stop_without_running_is_idempotent` asserting 200 + empty
  status, and add `loop_stop_clears_registry_even_when_harness_unreachable`
  that points a live registry entry at a dead port and asserts
  the registry is cleared and `loop_stopped` is broadcast.
- Update `AutomationBar.test.tsx` assertions to reflect the
  `(projectId, agentInstanceId)` signature and add a test for the
  rejected-stop path verifying the error modal appears and the
  Run button becomes enabled again.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Make Stop automation resilient to harness failures

Clarifies the orchestrator rule so the model emits the actual draft
markdown as visible assistant text rather than meta-commentary like
"I will create a spec", and reinforces the same expectation in the
create_spec and update_spec tool descriptions.

rawCommits.subject: Persist bootstrap ?host= query param into localStorage
rawCommits.body:
Desktop and native shells bootstrap the webview with a `?host=` query
param, but SPA navigation drops that query string on subsequent route
changes, causing later requests to fall back to a stale stored origin.
Sync the bootstrap host into localStorage at app startup, before any
module that reads the host origin, so the bootstrap value wins.

Made-with: Cursor

org-agnostic "Your active subscription".

rawCommits.subject: Preserve terminal task status across listTasks refetches too
rawCommits.body:
The previous commit guarded the `task_saved` event path, but the task
list was still reverting to `in_progress` during an automation run
because `refetchTasks` (and the `initialTasks` effect) replaced
`localTasks` with whatever `api.listTasks` returned, bypassing the
monotonic guard.

`refetchTasks` fires between every super-agent streaming turn (see the
`streamingId` effect), so any time the DB hadn't yet been transitioned
to `done` when the next turn ended, the client would clobber its own
`done` state — which `TaskList.displayStatus` then rendered as an open
circle because the underlying status was `in_progress` but the task
wasn't `liveTaskIds` anymore.

Extract the terminal-preservation merge into `mergeTaskPreservingTerminal`
and apply it to both `refetchTasks` and the `initialTasks` effect via
`mergeTaskListPreservingTerminal`, so a client-side `done`/`failed` is
never downgraded by a stale server snapshot.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Preserve terminal task status across listTasks refetches too

rawCommits.subject: Keep chat anchor pinned while resizing the sidekick pane
rawCommits.body:
During an active resize session, useScrollAnchorV2 only compensated for
per-frame height changes when the user was pinned to the bottom. When
scrolled up, the captured anchor was held but never restored until the
resize ended, so remeasured messages slid around as the divider moved.
Generalize scheduleResizePinnedFollow into scheduleResizeFollow so every
mid-drag content-height change either follows the bottom (pinned) or
restores the captured anchor (reading), keeping visible text stable.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Keep chat anchor pinned while resizing the sidekick pane

rawCommits.subject: Preserve terminal task status in the Kanban store
rawCommits.body:
The Kanban board dropped done/failed tasks back to earlier statuses when
entering the task app mid-automation because `addTask` and `fetchTasks`
replaced cached tasks with whatever the DB currently reported. Storage
snapshots from `task_saved` broadcasts and `listTasks` refetches can lag
the authoritative `task_completed`/`task_failed` WS events, so the client
already knows a task is terminal when a stale snapshot arrives.

Mirror the `preserveTerminalStatus` guard already used in
`useTaskListData` and `sidekick-store` so the Kanban cache honors
previously observed terminal statuses.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Preserve terminal task status in the Kanban store

rawCommits.subject: Preserve terminal task status in the Sidekick TaskList cache
rawCommits.body:
The Sidekick tasks tab reads its initial list from the react-query layout
cache, which was updated on every `task_saved` WS event with
`mergeTaskIntoProjectLayout` and never touched by the authoritative
`task_completed` / `task_failed` events. So a stale snapshot could
silently revert a Done task to `in_progress`, and a completion that
landed while the tab was unmounted never reached the cache at all. When
the user entered the Sidekick tasks tab mid-automation the TaskList
re-rendered from that stale cache and checked tasks appeared to lose
their state.

Guard `mergeTaskIntoProjectLayout` with the same terminal-status
preservation used in `useTaskListData`, `kanban-store`, and
`sidekick-store`, and add a `patchTaskStatusInProjectLayout` helper.
Subscribe to `TaskStarted` / `TaskCompleted` / `TaskFailed` in
`useProjectLayoutData` so authoritative transitions update the layout
cache regardless of whether the Sidekick TaskList is mounted.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Preserve terminal task status in the Sidekick TaskList cache

rawCommits.subject: Keep pinned chat at bottom during sidekick resize
rawCommits.body:
Follow-up to the anchor fix: when the user is pinned to the bottom, the
per-message ResizeObserver fires before React commits the virtualizer's
new totalSize, so doScrollToBottom was using a stale scrollHeight and
the view slid up for one frame. Run the resize correction synchronously
(no rAF) and also re-run it from a useLayoutEffect keyed on totalSize in
ChatMessageList so it fires once more post-commit but still pre-paint,
keeping the bottom and the reading-mode anchor perfectly stable while
dragging.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Keep pinned chat at bottom during sidekick resize
